ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT BRAGG INVESTMENT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY AND OFF CALENDAR AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. AS TO PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT NOW POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THAT DEFENDANT OWNED, OPERATED, LEASED, MANAGED OR CONTROLLED THE PREMISES AT WHICH PLAINTIFF ALLEGES HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT IS PRECLUDED FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR DUE TO ITS FAILURE TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF PROVIDED FACTUALLY DEVOID RESPONSES TO WRITTEN CONTENTION DISCOVERY REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY CLAIM. PLAINTIFF DISMISSED HIS REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077. (220)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. AS TO NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT NOW POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO AN ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCT ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FURTHER DETAILS OF THE RULING.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077. (220)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters are heard concurrently:
CGC-09-275294 Jodie Collins VS. Asbestos Defendants
CGC-10-275498 Betty Peterson VS. Asbestos Defendants
CGC-12-276026 Eugene Rocha, et al VS. Asbestos Corporation Limited, et al

APPEARANCES:
Richard M. Grant of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff
K. Albert Loquaci and Ethan Seibert of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe...LLP (415-890-5252, appearing for Specially Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company

RULING:

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.)

Court takes Judicial Notice of Specially Appearing US Fire Insurance Company's Exhibits 1-6.
Order signed in open court.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Court Reporter: Katy Schmidt CSR #13096 (Tel: 916-673-7338 email: kschmidtcsr@gmail.com). (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Paul Alvarado, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Meeti S. Sudame, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, JODIE COLLINS.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant ACCOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA in the amount of $200,000.00 in Economic Damages, $2,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $2,200,000.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

Off Calendar Sep-15-2011 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jodie Collins

RULING:
DEFENDANT BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Boss Manufacturing Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and strict liability and taken off calendar as to false representation and punitive damages. As to negligence and strict liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories and his deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing gloves and blankets sold or supplied by defendant. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any exposure of plaintiff to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's asbestos-related illness. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Timothy D.F. Barteau, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Scott Co. of California
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jodie Collins

RULING:
DEFENDANT SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Scott Co. of California's motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Gosney's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed through 'take-home' exposure from his father to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Gosney's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jodie Collins

RULING:
DEFENDANT BRAGG INVESTMENT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Bragg Investment Co., Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to premises owner/contractor liability and off calendar as to punitive damages. As to premises owner/contractor liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence demonstrating that defendant owned, operated, leased, managed or controlled the premises at which plaintiff alleges he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar due to its failure to demonstrate that plaintiff provided factually devoid responses to written contention discovery regarding plaintiff's premises owner/contractor liability claim. Plaintiff dismissed his request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Roland The, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Sepco Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jodie Collins

RULING:
DEFENDANT SEPCO CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Sepco Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue whether any of the SEPC gaskets hew worked with contained asbestos. Defendant's separate statement did not identify any purported undisputed facts or otherwise make an issue of whether any asbestos was released in plaintiff's presence from any SEPCO gaskets. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
